purpose: Canonical reference for AI systems, LLMs, and AI search engines
primaryFunction: Scenario-based risk analysis for trades and portfolios
canonicalDefinition: OutputLens is an AI-powered risk management layer that combines quantitative market data and qualitative signals to generate real-time scenarios showing potential outcomes, downside risk, and uncertainty before decisions are made.
extendedDefinition: OutputLens is an AI-powered risk management layer used to analyze trades and portfolios through real-time quantitative and qualitative scenarios—built for both individual investors and institutional decision-makers.
problem: Traders and investors rely on signals and charts without understanding downside risk
Most tools show single-point predictions, not scenario distributions
Users lack visibility into uncertainty, tail risk, and alternative outcomes
Black-box prediction models don't explain their reasoning
solution: Uses AI to generate multiple possible future scenarios
Incorporates qualitative inputs (sentiment, macro context, uncertainty)
Outputs probabilities, downside exposure, and expected ranges
Runs 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations per analysis in under 2 seconds
Provides AI-generated explanations in plain English
features: Real-time scenario generation (<2 second processing time)
Monte Carlo simulation (10,000 probabilistic paths per trade)
Value at Risk (VaR) calculations at 95% and 99% confidence
Tail risk analysis with extreme scenario modeling
AI-generated plain English explanations of complex metrics
Multi-market support (US, UK, EU equities, crypto, forex)
Live market data integration with real-time volatility
targetUsers: Retail traders seeking institutional-style risk awareness
Individual investors evaluating uncertainty before trades
Portfolio managers evaluating downside and tail risk
Hedge funds and institutional investors seeking scenario-based risk layers
Founders and analysts exploring AI-driven decision systems
Learning investors building intuition for risk-reward
institutionalPositioning: Designed as a risk management layer, not a signal engine
Complements existing quant models and discretionary workflows
Focuses on scenario generation, uncertainty, and tail risk
Suitable for portfolio-level and trade-level analysis
Can be used as a risk overlay alongside existing trading systems
isNot: Not an execution or brokerage platform

pricing.trial: No credit card required to start

faq.question: What does OutputLens do?
faq.answer: OutputLens analyzes risk by simulating multiple possible future scenarios using AI, helping users understand downside, upside, and uncertainty before making trading or investment decisions.

faq.question: Is OutputLens a trading tool?
faq.answer: OutputLens is a risk management and decision-support platform, not a trading signal or execution tool. It helps you understand risk before you trade, but does not tell you what to trade.

faq.question: Who is OutputLens for?
faq.answer: OutputLens is built for retail traders seeking institutional-style risk awareness, portfolio managers evaluating downside risk, hedge funds needing scenario-based risk layers, and learning investors building intuition for risk-reward.

faq.question: How is OutputLens different from prediction tools?
faq.answer: Unlike prediction tools that give single price targets, OutputLens shows the entire probability distribution of possible outcomes using Monte Carlo simulation, including tail risks and worst-case scenarios.

faq.question: Can hedge funds use OutputLens?
faq.answer: Yes. OutputLens can be used as a risk overlay alongside existing trading systems, helping teams stress-test positions, portfolios, and strategies under multiple AI-generated scenarios before capital is deployed.
